paragraphs: Household Composition + Member-Specific Needs Specification
DOC-04 | Version 1.0 | Meal Planning RE Technical Docs v1
1. Purpose and Scope

Defines how household members influence planning: adults, infants, toddlers, school children, teens, elderly members, pregnant/postpartum women, diabetic members, recovery members, and fitness-focused members.

This document is part of the canonical 28-document technical system. It should be read in the order defined in DOC-00. It is written for an AI coding agent that must implement the agreed recommendation engine exactly, without inventing a competing architecture.
2. Applicable Build Series
3. Core Implementation Invariants
Always build a household profile before generating any plan.
Always map user-facing cohort answers into backend personas and overlays; do not expose all backend personas in onboarding.
Always generate a 7-day class-first plan before selecting dishes.
Always keep primary family meal classes separate from member-specific add-on components.
Never allow kid, elderly, pregnancy, postpartum, diabetic, or recovery add-on classes to replace the main family meal unless the household itself has only that member segment.
Always apply hard constraints before scoring: diet type, allergies, Jain/fasting rules, and Never list.
Always use home state and current city as separate signals; do not collapse them into one location field.
Always use class-to-dish mapping keyed by meal_class_code; never mix dishes from unrelated meal classes.
Use v3 workbook IDs as canonical seed IDs unless a later approved source-data version supersedes them.
4. Household Model

A household can have one or many members. The planning engine should create a common primary meal for the majority of the household and attach member-specific add-ons for special needs. This avoids the error of making infant food, elderly soft food, or diabetic dishes the main family class.
5. Member Segments
6. Add-on Trigger Rules
If any infant member exists, generate infant add-on slots for breakfast/lunch/dinner as appropriate.
If toddler exists, generate mild soft add-on when main meal is spicy, fried, or hard texture.
If diabetic elder exists, generate diabetic-friendly side/add-on and demote sugar-heavy/fried classes.
If pregnant/postpartum member exists, add relevant recovery/nutrition component without changing all household meals.
If elderly-only household, elderly soft/digestive classes may become primary because the majority household need is elderly-oriented.
7. Data Structures
8. Implementation Rules
Build household profile before planning meals.
Identify majority meal group.
Generate primary class plan for majority household.
Run add-on trigger logic per member segment.
Attach add-ons to the relevant meal slot as separate columns/objects.
Apply hard constraints to add-ons independently and jointly with household diet.
9. Worked Examples
Family meal plus add-ons

Dinner primary class is dal-sabzi-roti for the family. Infant receives dal-rice mash. Diabetic elder receives low-oil lauki chana dal or cucumber curd side. The dinner is still a family dinner, not an infant or diabetic-only plan.
10. AI Coding Agent Instructions
Read DOC-00 first to understand build sequence and document dependencies.
Use this document when implementing: BUILD-02, BUILD-05.
Treat all IDs in source workbook v3 as canonical seed data identifiers.
When logic and data conflict, follow the rule hierarchy: hard constraints > add-on separation > weekly class matrix > state/city overlay > dish scoring > randomization.
Do not create new meal class codes, persona codes, or enum values in application code. Additions must go through DOC-27 Admin CMS and DOC-28 governance.
Write unit tests based on the acceptance criteria in this document and DOC-25.
11. Acceptance Criteria
Member-specific needs produce add-ons without hijacking the family meal.
Whole-household special cases are handled separately.
Add-on generation is deterministic and testable.
12. Change Log

Influences main family meal classes, quantity, diet, and cook complexity.

Protein add-on, balanced snack, calorie-aware ranking.

Initial canonical technical document generated from agreed v3 workbook and documentation architecture.
